phases.label: Winter reset
phases.summary: Minimal turf work. Service equipment, review prior season, and decide whether a soil test is needed.
phases.leadTime: Buy mower blade/sharpening service or soil test materials before spring growth begins.

phases.label: Spring wake-up
phases.summary: Start mowing when growth begins. Keep irrigation light and avoid heavy early fertilizer.
phases.leadTime: Purchase crabgrass pre-emergent before the redbud/full-bloom application window if prevention is planned.

phases.label: Spring prevention
phases.summary: Mow regularly, apply crabgrass prevention only if appropriate, and mark thin areas for fall.
phases.leadTime: Use the 8,225 sq ft lawn area to plan pre-emergent purchases. Watch rain and wind before applying product.

phases.label: Late spring transition
phases.summary: Light fertilization only if irrigation and summer care are realistic. Watch heat, weeds, and early disease pressure.
phases.leadTime: Confirm irrigation coverage before depending on fertilizer response.

phases.label: Summer protection
phases.summary: Mow high, water only as needed, and avoid forcing cool-season turf through Kansas heat.
phases.leadTime: If a second crabgrass preventer is needed by label timing, plan by early June.

phases.label: Fall prep
phases.summary: Plan seed, aeration, soil testing, and fertilizer. Treat grubs only if confirmed.
phases.leadTime: Buy quality turf-type tall fescue seed and fall fertilizer before September demand.

phases.label: Fall build
phases.summary: Core aerate if needed, seed thin areas, fertilize, and keep new seed consistently moist.
phases.leadTime: Have seed, fertilizer, and watering plan ready before the first good September window.

phases.label: Winterizer and closeout
phases.summary: Apply high-nitrogen fall fertilizer, manage leaves, store equipment, and review what worked.
phases.leadTime: Buy winterizer before the late fall window and avoid scrambling after leaf cleanup begins.
